anti_fund_growth.description: Geoffrey Woo's affiliated private tech crossover fund, shown below the public-stock portfolio weights. Growth I is not a public-stock fund. Public weights use mark-to-market estimates; share counts, PPS, dollar marks, account records, and raw LP model fields are excluded.

positions.mark_basis: Series C and prior-position model mark

methodology.caveat: 13F filings do not disclose true entry price, intraday trading, shorts, private marks, or post-quarter changes.
methodology.manager_current_value_proxy: Latest reported 13F common-stock units multiplied by latest available free daily close.
methodology.manager_entry_proxy: Observed 13F share-delta weighted average. First observed position uses the quarter-end 13F implied mark; later additions use the quarter-end mark for added units; reductions reduce units at the running proxy basis.
methodology.portfolio_entry_proxy: Broker cost basis divided by private position units when available; otherwise latest position mark is treated as a weak proxy.

components.rule: Growth durability, margin trajectory, cash generation, balance sheet, capex ROIC, revisions, valuation, and falsifiers drive the first 60% of the decision stack.
Sector-specific KPI templates and AI-max tailwinds/headwinds drive the next 20% of the decision stack.
13F ownership is a tertiary prior and confirmation signal; it cannot create an add without bottom-up evidence.
Macro, timing, earnings proximity, and liquidity/rates pressure affect timing, drawdown risk, and max allowed size.
Bull/base/bear 12-month scenarios create a probability-weighted forward return anchored by company and sector work.
Concentration, bucket exposure, crowding, hard-risk events, put tilt, and extreme price moves penalize target size.
Risk-adjusted expected return converts to normalized model target weights; every add must be funded by named trims, cash reserve draw, or both before publication.

promotion_rules: Every ranked ticker receives a company underwriting row, sector row, versioned feature row, and scenario study record.
A ticker cannot receive Add unless company underwriting clears the bottom-up evidence floor.
Strong 13F ownership without company evidence produces Watch or Study, not Add.
Company deterioration can trigger Trim even when manager ownership remains strong.
Higher target weights require positive risk-adjusted expected return, company evidence quality, sector support, and timing.
Weak valuation support, stale evidence, crowding, earnings proximity, and drawdown risk reduce target size.
Every trade item carries current weight, post-action target, model target weight, delta, funding source, constraints, catalyst clock, company reason, sector reason, tertiary signals, and falsifier.

methodology.summary: AlloIQ ranks the AI-max universe company-first, then sector setup, then manager 13F and macro timing, with ML-ready features and forward outcome tracking.
